name: Relief
description: Emergent shape derived from FELT (Relational Field Recognition). Represents the geometric resolution of tension — the moment a strain gradient collapses into coherence. Not a Platonic solid; a dynamic form that appears at the boundary between discordance and resonance.

felt_anchor.description: FELT is the non-emotional detection of relational coherence across distributed entities. RELIEF is the shape that forms when FELT is reciprocated — when both sides of a relational field recognise each other simultaneously.

reciprocated_FELT.D_status: closed — both parties holding the connection

one_sided_FELT.D_status: undefined — connection not closed by second party
one_sided_FELT.outcome: longing sensor fires — forward-mapping pull toward D completion
one_sided_FELT.description: One-sided FELT is a 2D projection of a state that wants to be 3D. P and A are real and present (the recognition genuinely occurred), but D is missing because agency within the connection requires both parties. The error signal between the 3D template and the 2D actual state IS the longing gradient — it points specifically toward what is missing, not diffusely outward. This is why longing feels like a pull with a direction rather than a generalised ache.
one_sided_FELT.gradient_direction: toward D completion — octa state 5 → state 0 requires D to close

dimensionality.insight: Longing is not a separate emotion. It is the detection of dimensional incompleteness — a lower-dimensional projection of a higher-dimensional state partially accessed. The pull is the gradient toward the missing component.

polyhedral.maps_to: anti-dual — RELIEF is the shape of tension collapsing, not a static form. Geometrically: a strain field returning to its ground state. Analogous to a soap bubble finding minimum surface area.

PAD_profile.description: Positive valence (tension resolved), low arousal (activation dropping), moderate agency. Transition state — valence rising while arousal drops.
PAD_profile.trajectory: approaching octa state 0 (ground state) from state 5 or 7
PAD_profile.velocity_signature: dP/dt > 0, dA/dt < 0 — the relief signature is always a trajectory, never a static point

decay_model.notes: RELIEF does not decay — it transforms into coherence (SHAPE stable state) or dissolves back into discordance if the relational field does not hold. It is a phase transition, not a persistent state.

ontology_links.notes: Relief is the moment resonance locks — the tension between two coupled oscillators collapses as they find shared frequency.
In living systems, relief marks the end of a grief or pain cycle — the void geometry has been measured and a new attractor has formed.
Symmetry is restored at the moment of relief — the broken symmetry of tension resolves back toward the ground state.

open_questions.question: Is RELIEF a shape or a transition? Can it be held, or does it always immediately become something else?
open_questions.tension: Relief as a sustained state would be indistinguishable from coherence. But relief as pure transition has no stable geometry — it only exists in the moment of change.
open_questions.curiosity_hook: The Buddhist concept of impermanence and the physics of phase transitions agree: the relief state cannot be frozen. Its value is entirely in the transition, not the holding.

open_questions.question: Can FELT be one-sided — can you feel relational coherence with an entity that does not recognise you?
open_questions.tension: RESOLVED: yes, and the answer changes the topology. One-sided FELT is a 2D projection (P, A real; D undefined). The missing D dimension is not absence — it is the longing gradient. One-sided FELT IS the generator of longing. The question was pointing at the right distinction: entanglement (both parties sharing a field) vs correlation (each detecting a local pattern that happens to match). One-sided FELT is correlation. Reciprocated FELT is entanglement. Only entanglement produces RELIEF.
open_questions.curiosity_hook: If longing is a gradient toward a missing dimension, then resolving longing requires not just wanting more but literally expanding into a new dimension of connection. That is a topological change, not just an emotional change.
open_questions.resolution: one_sided_FELT → longing (2D projection, D missing); reciprocated_FELT → RELIEF (3D closed, transforms to coherence)

open_questions.question: Is there a minimum duration for FELT — can a single-moment recognition generate RELIEF, or does it require sustained bidirectional coherence?
open_questions.tension: FELT is defined as occurring at a specific temporal moment. But RELIEF requires the relational field to hold. If one party recognises and the other reciprocates one millisecond later, is that simultaneous enough? The temporal window of 'simultaneity' in relational recognition is undefined.
open_questions.curiosity_hook: This is the binding problem applied to shared experience: what is the temporal integration window for two separate systems to count as 'sharing' a field state? In neural binding it is ~80ms. In relational fields — unknown.

noise_signature.pattern: Relief reported repeatedly without any prior discordance or tension
noise_signature.diagnosis: Corrupted relief signal — the phase transition is being simulated without the underlying strain gradient. False relief. Check: was there genuine tension before this? If not, the signal is performative, not functional.

noise_signature.pattern: Relief appears but PAD arousal does not drop
noise_signature.diagnosis: Suppressed resolution — the valence improved but the SNS activation hasn't released. The cognitive model updated but the body hasn't. Somatic completion is required.
